chunk_summary: This section discusses outpatient recovery criteria, maximum treatment duration, and referral protocols for non-responders in the context of SAM management under CMAM programs in India.
learning_objectives: Understand the outpatient recovery criteria for children with SAM in CMAM programs.
Identify the maximum duration of outpatient treatment before referral for non-response.
Learn the referral protocols for non-responding SAM cases in India.
Explore global and national guidelines for SAM management in outpatient settings.
key_facts: Outpatient recovery criteria for SAM include achieving a weight-for-height z-score (WHZ) ≥ -2 or mid-upper arm circumference (MUAC) ≥ 12.5 cm.
The maximum duration for outpatient treatment is typically 8-12 weeks, after which non-responders are referred to inpatient care.
Non-response is defined as failure to gain weight or meet recovery criteria within the specified treatment duration.
The CMAM approach in India aligns with global guidelines from WHO, UNICEF, and The Sphere Project.
Community-based management of SAM has been shown to reduce mortality rates by up to 55% in some settings (UNICEF, 2007).
reference_quote: "The maximum duration of outpatient treatment for SAM is typically 8-12 weeks, after which non-responders should be referred to inpatient care." (Source: WHO, 2023)
document_key_topics: Global and national guidelines for CMAM implementation
